sections.paragraph:
f. [root בִּין].-

(1) understanding; Isa 33:19, “a people of strange language אֵין בִּינָה whom thou dost not understand;” Dan 8:15 9:22 10:1 .

(2) intelligence (Einficht, Berftand), insight, Pro 4:5, 8:14 9:6 , 10 16:16 Job 28:12, 20 Job 28:20יָדַע בִּינָה “to be or to become intelligent,” Job 38:4 Pro 4:1 Isa 29:24. Pl. עַם בִּינֹות “an intelligent people,” Isa 27:11. Specially skill, in any art or learning, 2Ch 2:12 1Ch 12:32, יֹדְעֵי בִינָה לָעִתִּים “skilled in understanding the times” (compare Est 1:13).

בינה

f., Ch. i.q. Heb. No. 2; Dan 2:21.
